description: Keeper hooks: (1) events-writer appends one per-pid NDJSON line per hook invocation (lock-free, no SQLite) for the keeperd ingester to fold into the jobs projection; (2) branch-guard is a PreToolUse(Bash) hard-deny that blocks subagents (agent_id present) from creating/switching git branches and from mutating git stash (list/show/create stay allowed) via the deny JSON envelope (still exits 0), keeping worker subagents on the current branch; (2b) escalation-guard is a PreToolUse(Bash) hard-deny that constrains an escalation session (KEEPER_ESCALATION_ROLE env marker set to unblock/resolve/deconflict/repair) to its role's Bash command-family allowlist — denying interpreter one-liners, heredocs, redirects, tee, command substitution, env-runner wrappers, and off-list/compound commands (the file-write bypass class that survives --dangerously-skip-permissions) via the deny JSON envelope; it fails CLOSED for a marked session and stays inert (fail-open) for an unmarked human/subagent session, always exiting 0; (2c) wrong-tree-guard is a PreToolUse(Write|Edit|MultiEdit|Bash) hard-deny that stops a worktree-LANE worker (KEEPER_PLAN_WORKTREE env marker set to the lane path) from writing into a tracked repo working tree that is not its own lane — resolving each write target (direct-tool file_path, and best-effort Bash redirect/heredoc/tee/sed -i vectors) and denying via the deny JSON envelope when it lands in a foreign .git-bounded tree, while allowing plan-state (.keeper), temp/home/state-dir, and own-lane writes and denying an unconditional .git/config + credential denylist regardless of lane; it FAILS OPEN on every internal error (best-effort audit, not a security boundary), stays inert for an unmarked session, and always exits 0; (2d) wrapped-guard is a PreToolUse(Write|Edit|MultiEdit|NotebookEdit|Bash) hard-deny that stops a wrapped-cell work:worker subagent (KEEPER_WRAPPED_CELL env marker non-empty AND agent_id/agent_type present) from editing source natively — a dumb-courier wrapper delegates all implementation to its provider leg via keeper agent run and owns only the keeper close-out, so this is a single-state total edit-denial: Edit/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit are denied outright, a Write is denied inside any tracked repo tree (out-of-tree writes like the scratchpad contract file pass), and Bash is a POSITIVE allowlist (reject the whole shell-operator/expansion/redirect/heredoc/substitution surface and re-entrant wrappers up front, then allow only keeper agent/commit-work/plan done+reads/session state, read-only + staging git, and the test runner) that denies every in-tree write vector as off-list; it FAILS CLOSED (deny) for a marked subagent on any unclearable command/payload and on parse ambiguity, stays inert (fail-open) for an unmarked human/native session, and always exits 0; (3) sidecar-writer is a fail-open PostToolUse(Write|Edit|MultiEdit|Bash) hook that owns the ~/docs metadata sidecar AND git state — create-or-merge a doc's .yaml on Write, upsert the gist-url on `gh gist create` (NEVER touching the .md), and commit every doc write/edit/delete to the ~/docs repo (pathspec-scoped, mechanical message, fail-open so a commit failure never blocks; still exits 0); (4) docs-pusher is a fail-open Stop hook that pushes ~/docs to its remote once per turn when local is ahead of @{u} (local ahead-count, no fetch), serialized by a .git/ lockfile and best-effort: a non-fast-forward/auth/network failure is logged to .git/keeper-push.log and skipped (never rebase, never --force) and it ALWAYS exits 0 — a Stop hook exiting non-zero would block Claude from stopping; (5) context-hint is a fail-open SessionStart hook that emits one additionalContext line pointing a session at the repo root's CONTEXT.md glossary when it is present and non-empty (absent/empty/unreadable/non-git cwd → emits nothing), a dep-free node-only fs walk that ALWAYS exits 0
